explanation:
**Mathematics** is a universal tool: algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics. Applied in engineering, science, finance, and everyday life.

## How It's Calculated

Linear regression finds the best-fit line through your data points using the least squares method. This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unexpected or unusual data.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What is R² in linear regression?
faq.a: R² measures goodness of fit, ranging from 0 (poor) to 1 (perfect). It indicates what percentage of variance your regression model explains.

faq.q: How do outliers affect linear regression?
faq.a: Outliers significantly impact the slope (m) and intercept (b). Consider removing them or using robust regression methods if they distort your results.

faq.q: Can I extrapolate beyond my data range?
faq.a: Extrapolation is risky. Predictions are reliable only within the range of your original data. Going beyond risks inaccurate results.

faq.q: What is the difference between r and R²?
faq.a: Correlation coefficient (r) is ±√R². It ranges from -1 to +1 and shows the direction and strength of the linear relationship.

faq.q: What should I do if my data isn't linear?
faq.a: Try transforming variables (logarithm, square root) or use polynomial regression. Check R² after transformation to see if it improves.
